question: Which of the following best describes the role of corneal scraping culture in the research methodology of the KeratitisDataset?

options.A: Corneal scraping culture is a secondary confirmation method for diagnosing patients already identified with keratitis through symptoms alone.
options.B: Corneal scraping culture is utilized to determine the genetic predisposition of patients towards developing keratitis.
options.C: Corneal scraping culture is primarily used to determine the effectiveness of different medication on keratitis treatment.
options.D: Corneal scraping culture is used to differentiate between healthy and non-healthy patients in the study of various keratitis types.

explanation: Corneal scraping culture, as a type of medical test, is utilized in the KeratitisDataset to confirm the keratitis condition (AK, BK, FK) in patients, thereby distinguishing between healthy and non-healthy patients. This aligns with the knowledge path from the dataset through the disease to the test type, indicating its role in confirming the disease among the studied subjects.
